title: ReplaceFaceFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing replace face features in a component and supports the ability to create new replace face features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new replace face feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the replace face you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified replace face feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified replace face feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of replace face features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: ReplaceFace Feature
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new replaceface feature.

add.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new replace face feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created ReplaceFaceFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object that defines the desired replace face. Use the createInput method to create a new ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object) to define the replace face.

related_samples.name: ReplaceFace Feature
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new replaceface feature.

classType.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the replace face you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created ReplaceFaceFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: sourceFaces
parameters.description: Input the entities that define the source faces (the faces to be replaced). The collection can contain the faces from a solid and/or features. All the faces must be on the same body.

parameters.name: isTangentChain
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not faces that are tangentially connected to the input faces (if any) will also be included. A value of true indicates that tangent faces will be included.

parameters.name: targetFaces
parameters.description: Input the entities that define the target faces. The new faces must completely intersect the part. The collection can contain the surface faces, surface bodies and construction planes.

related_samples.name: ReplaceFace Feature
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new replaceface feature.

item.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified replace face feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified replace face feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of replace face features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = replaceFaceFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = replaceFaceFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: ReplaceFaceFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = replaceFaceFeatures_var.objectType
